title: Sloanea sterculiacea
intro: Sloanea sterculiacea, also known as the West African mango or African mango, is a tropical tree native to West Africa. The fruit and bark of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of conditions such as diabetes, obesity, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Sloanea sterculiacea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, the fruit and bark of Sloanea sterculiacea are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and folk medicines.
taxonomy: Sloanea sterculiacea is classified within the family Ebenaceae. This species belongs to the genus Sloanea, which is a group of flowering plants. The family Ebenaceae is a small family of flowering plants that are commonly found in tropical regions. Sloanea sterculiacea is native to tropical Asia and the Pacific Islands. It is a member of the order Celastrales, which is an order of flowering plants that are primarily woody in nature.
uses: Sloanea sterculiacea, also known as the Yellow Sap tree, has been utilized in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various ailments. In some cultures, Sloanea sterculiacea is used to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions. The fruit, leaves, and bark of the tree are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and digestive problems.
